references.text: Backer, CA & RC Bakhuizen van den Brink. 1968. Flora of Java 3: 12-22.
Brennan, JPM. 1966. The classification of Commelinaceae. Botanical Journal of the Linnean Society 59: 349-370.
Duistermaat, H. 2012. A taxonomic revision of Amischotolype (Commelinaceae) in Asia. Gardens' Bulletin Singapore 64: 51–131
Faden, RB. 1989. Commelina caroliniana (Commelinaceae): A misunderstood species in the United States is an old introduction from Asia. Taxon 38: 43-53.
Lee, C-K, S Fuse, M Poopath, R Pooma & MN Tamura. 2021. Phylogenetics and infrafamilial classification of Commelinaceae (Commelinales). Botanical Journal of the Linnean Society 198: 117-130.
Nampy, S, SM Joseph & KM Manudev. 2013. The genus Commelina (Commelinaceae) in Andaman & Nicobar Islands, India with one new species and three new records. Phytotaxa 87: 19-29
Wang JC, Chen YJ & Peng CI. 2000. Commelinaceae. Flora of Taiwan 2nd. edition 5: 153-178.

species.citation: (A.Rich.) D.Y.Hong , Acta Phytotax. Sin. 12 (1974) 461; ≡ Forrestia hispida A.Rich. , Voy. Astrolabe 2 (1834) 2; Type: from New Guinea. = Forrestia hispida var. philippinensis (Merr.) Merr. , EPFP 1 (1923) 197; ≡ Forrestia philippinensis Merr. , Publ. Bur. Sci. Gov. Lab. 35 (1906) 5; Type: Merrill 4051 (PNH, holo; K, photo iso).
species.distribution: China, Indochina, Java, New Guinea, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Taiwan. BASILAN, BOHOL (photos), CATANDUANES, CEBU, LEYTE, LUZON: Albay, Camarines Sur, Laguna, Nueva Vizcaya, Quezon, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Agusan del Sur, Bukidnon, Davao Oriental (photos), Davao del Norte, Davao del Sur, Lanao, Lanao del Sur (photos), Misamis Oriental (photos), Surigao del Norte (photos), Surigao del Sur, Zamboanga del Sur (photos), MINDORO, NEGROS, PANAY, POLILLO, SAMAR, TAWI-TAWI.
species.notes: Low and medium elevation forests. Thickets and old clearings at the vicinity of primary forests. 120-2000m.

species.citation: (Blume) Hassk. , Flora 46 (1863) 392; ≡ Campelia marginata Blume , Enum. Pl. Javae (1827) 7; Type: from Indonesia.
species.distribution: Borneo, Java, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Philippines, Singapore, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand. MINDANAO: Lanao del Sur, PALAWAN.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 2 June 2026

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 41 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 138; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 195.
species.distribution: Africa, Andaman Isls, Bangladesh, Cambodia, China, India, Indian Ocean, Japan, Java, Korea, Malay Peninsula, Middle East, Myanmar, Nepal, Nicobar Isls, Pakistan, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Sri Lanka, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam. BASILAN, LEYTE, LUZON: Bulacan, Cavite, Ifugao, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, Laguna, Mountain Province, NCR, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, South Cotabato, MINDORO (photos), PALAWAN, PANAY (photos).
species.notes: Throughout the Philippines. Lowland and medium elevation grasslands, waste places in the settled areas. Often common. 15-1656m.

species.citation: Walter , Fl. Carol. (1788) 68 ; Faden, Taxon 38 (1989) 50; Nampy et al., Phytotaxa 87 (2013) 26.
species.distribution: Bangladesh, India, Nepal.
species.notes: Note by P.B. Pelser (17-Jan-21): I did not find notes on the ecology of this species for the Philippines, but elsewhere it is reported from paddy fields and along roadsides, ditches, and streams (Nampy et al. 2013).

species.citation: Burm.f. , Fl. Ind. (N. L. Burman) (1768) 18 ; Romeroso et al., Asian J. Biol. Life Sc. 10 (2021) 437. ssp. diffusa. = Commelina luzonensis Elmer, LPB 9 (1934) 3184; Type: Elmer 22174 (K, NY, US, iso), Luzon: Pampanga, Camp Stotesnburg (Mt Pinatubo), thickets of moist ravines, 3500ft, 27-May-1927. Distribution: Pantropic. LUZON: Pampanga. Notes: Throughout the Philippines. Low and medium elevation grasslands, waste places, etc. Native.
species.distribution: BASILAN, BATAN, CEBU, HOMONHON, LEYTE, LUZON: Abra (photos), Bataan, Benguet, Bulacan, Camarines Norte, Ifugao, Ilocos Sur, Mountain Province, NCR, Pampanga, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Bukidnon, Davao Oriental, Davao de Oro, Davao del Sur, Misamis Oriental (photos), South Cotabato, Zamboanga, MINDORO, ?PANAY, SAMAR.
species.notes: J.E.M. Villasis (31-May-26): also present in Panay. 0-1286m.

species.citation: Blume , Enum. Pl. Javae (1827) 3.
species.distribution: Bangladesh, China, India, Java, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Nepal, Philippines, Thailand. CULION, PALAWAN.
species.notes: Record from Culion: Merrill 531, US.

species.citation: R.Br. , Prodr. Fl. Nov. Holland. (1810) 270 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 195.
species.distribution: Australia, Bangladesh, China, India, Indian Ocean, Moluccas, Pakistan, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Sri Lanka, Taiwan, Vietnam. LUZON: NCR.
species.notes: Record from Luzon: Loher s.n., US.

species.citation: Klotzsch ex C.B.Clarke , Monogr. Phan. 3 (1881) 186 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 195.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines.
species.notes: “The type is a specimen from Luzon, collected by Chamisso, probably from either Cavite or Batangas Province or possibly from the vicinity of Manila. I have seen no specimens I can with certainty refer to it, but some may have been distributed sub C. nudiflora L.” Merr. (1922: 195).

species.citation: R.Br. , Prodr. Fl. Nov. Holland. (1810) 269.
species.distribution: Australia, India, New Guinea, ?Philippines, Sri Lanka. ?BATAN, ?LUZON: ?Rizal.
species.notes: Possibly represented in the Philippines by Mendoza PNH20449 (PNH) from Rizal, and Quisumbing, Del Rosario & Gutierrez PNH79473 (PNH) from Batan Isl. 200m.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 1 September 2024

species.citation: (L.) D.Don ex Sweet , Hort. Brit. (Sweet), 1st Ed. (1826) 430 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 140; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 198; ≡ Amischophacelus axillaris (L.) R.S.Rao & Kammathy , J. Linn. Soc., Bot. 59 (1966) 306; ≡ Commelina axillaris L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 42.
species.distribution: Australia, Bangladesh, Cambodia, China, India, Java, Laos, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Nepal, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Taiwan, Vietnam. BALABAC, BOHOL, CEBU, LUZON: Bataan, Kalinga, Laguna, NCR, Pangasinan, Quezon, Rizal, MASBATE, MINDANAO: Davao, MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAWAN, PANAY, SAMAR.
species.notes: Throughout the Philippines in most islands and provinces. Low to medium elevation in clearings, open places along streams, rice paddies, etc. 5m.

species.citation: (Blume) Bakh.f. , Blumea 6 (1950) 399; ≡ Belosynapsis ciliata (Blume) R.S.Rao , Notes Roy. Bot. Gard. Edinburgh 25 (1964) 187; ≡ Tradescantia ciliata Blume , Cat. Gew. Buitenzorg (1823) 61. = Cyanotis capitata (Blume) C.B.Clarke , Monogr. Phan. 3 (1881) 243 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 198; ≡ Tradescantia capitata Blume , Enum. Pl. Javae (1827) 6.
species.distribution: China, India, Japan, Java, New Guinea, Philippines, Taiwan. BASILAN, CAGAYAN DE SULU, CEBU, LEYTE, LUZON: Abra (photos), Bataan, Benguet, Ifugao, Laguna, Pampanga (photos), Rizal, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Davao Oriental, Misamis Oriental (photos), Zamboanga, Zamboanga del Norte (photos), Zamboanga del Sur, MINDORO, PALAWAN, PANAY, SAMAR.

species.citation: (L.) D.Don , Prodr. Fl. Nepal. (1825) 46 ; Roem. & Schult., Syst. 72 (1830) 1150; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 140; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 198; ≡ Commelina cristata L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 42.
species.distribution: India, Indian Ocean to Malesia. BASILAN, LUZON: Bataan, Benguet, Ilocos Norte, Laguna, NCR, Pampanga (photos), Pangasinan, Rizal, Zambales, MINDORO, PALAWAN (photos), PANAY (photos).
species.notes: In most or all provinces in Luzon. Old clearings, open places along streams, about cliffs, etc. At low and medium elevation. 60-400m.

species.citation: (Roxb.) Merr. , PJS 2c (1907) 266 ; Fl. Manila (1912) 139; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 199; ≡ Belosynapsis moluccana (Roxb.) C.E.C.Fisch. , Bull. Misc. Inform. Kew 1928 (1928) 406; ≡ Commelina moluccana Roxb. , Fl. Ind. (Carey & Wallich) 1 (1820) 176.
species.distribution: Borneo, Java, Moluccas, Philippines. JOLO, LEYTE, LUZON: Bataan, Ifugao, Laguna, Mountain Province, NCR, Pampanga, Rizal, Sorsogon, MINDANAO, MINDORO.
species.notes: Low and medium elevation, on boulders in ravines, along small streams, etc.

species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LEYTE.

species.citation: J.C.Mikan , Del. Fl. Faun. Bras. (1820) 3.
species.distribution: S America. LUZON: Batangas (photos).
species.notes: Samuel C. Brillo (14-Jul-24): Possibly escaped from cultivation and naturalized between a forest edge and a resort. Naturalized in Java.

species.citation: Lour. , Fl. Cochinch. (1790) 193 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 199.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Australia, Bangladesh, Borneo, Cambodia, China, India, Java, Laos, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Nepal, Nicobar Isls, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Taiwan, Thailand, Tibet, Vietnam. BASILAN, BILIRAN, CALAMIANES, CULION, LEYTE, LUZON: Abra (photos), Albay, Bataan, Benguet, Bulacan, Cagayan, Camarines, Camarines Sur, Laguna (photos), Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Bukidnon, Lanao del Sur, Zamboanga Sibugay, MINDORO, NEGROS (photos), PALAWAN (photos), SAMAR, SIBUYAN.
species.notes: N Luzon to the Calamianes and Mindanao, in most or all islands and provinces. In wet places along small streams at low and medium elevation. 0-700m (photos).

species.citation: (Stokes) Faden , Taxon 29 (1980) 77; ≡ Commelina edulis Stokes , Bot. Mat. Med. 1 (1812) 184.
species.distribution: Bangladesh, Cambodia, China, India, Java, Java Sea, Laos, Lesser Sunda Isls, Myanmar, Nepal, New Guinea, Philippines, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam. LUZON: NCR, Pangasinan, Rizal.
species.notes: Lowlands, in shaded places along streams.

species.citation: (Vahl) G.Brückn. , Nat. Pflanzenfam. (Engler & Prantl), 2nd Ed. 15a (1930) 173; ≡ Commelina gigantea Vahl , Enum. Pl. (Vahl) 2 (1805) 177.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Australia, Bangladesh, Borneo, Cambodia, India, Java, Madagascar, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, New Guinea, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Vietnam. MINDANAO: Bukidnon, PALAWAN, SEMIRARA.
species.notes: Low and medium elevation open grasslands.

species.citation: (Hassk.) R.S.Rao & Kammathy , Bull. Bot. Surv. India 3 (1962) 393; ≡ Aneilema loriforme Hassk. , Pl. Jungh. (1852) 143.
species.distribution: Bangladesh, China, India, Japan, Java, New Guinea, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Sri Lanka, Taiwan, Thailand, Tibet, Vietnam. LEYTE, LUZON: Abra (photos), Apayao (photos), MINDANAO: Davao, MINDORO (photos), NEGROS.
species.notes: 900m (photos).

species.citation: (L.) Brenan , Kew Bull. 7 (1952) 189 ; Raganas et al., Biodiversitas 26 (2025) 1194; ≡ Commelina nudiflora L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 41.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Australia, Bangladesh, Bismarck Arch, Borneo, Cambodia, China, India, Java, Laos, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Nepal, New Guinea, Nicobar Isls, Pacific Ocean, Pakistan, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Sri Lanka, Thailand, Vietnam. BUSUANGA, LUZON: Abra (photos), MINDANAO: Davao del Norte, PALAWAN.
species.notes: Invasive species (CABI 2017). Throughout the Philippines. In open grasslands, waste places, etc. Common. Sea level and low and medium elevation. 50-1100m (photos).

species.citation: (Kurz) Faden , Taxon 29 (1980) 74; ≡ Aneilema spectabile Kurz , J. Asiat. Soc. Bengal, Pt. 2, Nat. Hist. 40 (1871) 77.
species.distribution: Cambodia, China, Laos, Myanmar, Philippines, Thailand, Vietnam. LUZON: Bulacan, SEMIRARA.
species.notes: Low elevation open wet grasslands.

species.citation: (L.) G.Brückn. , Nat. Pflanzenfam. (Engler & Prantl), 2nd Ed. 15a (1930) 173; ≡ Commelina spirata L. , Mant. Pl. Altera (1771) 176. var. spirata. Notes: According to Merrill (1922), credited to the Philippines by C.B. Clarke on the basis of a specimen collected by Baume (P) from Luzon: Laguna de Bay. “This specimen may be A[neilema] malabaricum (L.) Merr., or I may have included A. spiratum R.Br. in my conception of A. malabaricum (L.) Merr.”. Native.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Bangladesh, China, India, Java, Laos, Myanmar, Nepal, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Taiwan, Vietnam.

species.citation: (L.) G.Brückn. , Nat. Pflanzenfam. (Engler & Prantl), 2nd Ed. 15a (1930) 173; ≡ Commelina vaginata L. , Mant. Pl. Altera (1771) 177. var. vaginata. Distribution: NEGROS. Native.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Australia, Bangladesh, Cambodia, China, India, Java, Nepal, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Thailand, Vietnam. LUZON: NCR, MINDORO, NEGROS.

species.citation: (Dalzell) G.Brückn. , Nat. Pflanzenfam. (Engler & Prantl), 2nd Ed. 15a (1930) 173; ≡ Aneilema versicolor Dalzell , Hooker's J. Bot. Kew Gard. Misc. 3 (1851) 136.
species.distribution: India, Philippines, Vietnam. CULION, JOLO, LUZON: NCR, Nueva Vizcaya, Rizal, ?MINDANAO: ?Zamboanga del Norte (photos), MINDORO (photos), PANAY (photos).
species.notes: Lowlands. Open wet grasslands, fallow rice paddies, etc. 600-870m.

species.citation: (R.Br.) Benth. , Fl. Austral. 7 (1878) 90; ≡ Aneilema macrophyllum R.Br. , Prodr. Fl. Nov. Holland. (1810) 270. = Aclisia cumingiana Hassk. , Commelin. Ind. (1870) 47.
species.distribution: Australia, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi. LUZON: Bataan, Batangas, Cagayan, Ilocos Norte, Laguna, Nueva Vizcaya, Pampanga, Quezon, Rizal, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Davao, Davao del Sur, NEGROS (photos), SAMAR.

species.citation: (Blume) Bakh.f. , Bekn. Fl. Java 211 (1949) 10; Type: from Indonesia. = Pollia sorzogonensis (E.Mey.) Steud. , Nomencl. Bot. (Steudel), 2nd Ed. 2 (1841) 368; ≡ Aclisia sorzogonensis E.Mey. , Reliq. Haenk. 1 (1827) 138.
species.distribution: Bangladesh, Bhutan, Cambodia, China, India, Java, Laos, Myanmar, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Sri Lanka, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam. BASILAN, BATAN, BILIRAN, CAOHAGAN DE SAMAR, CEBU, JOLO, LEYTE, LUZON: Albay, Batangas, Bulacan, Cagayan, Camarines Sur, Cavite, Ilocos Norte, Kalinga, La Union, Laguna, NCR, Nueva Ecija, Pampanga, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Davao, Davao Oriental, Davao del Sur, Zamboanga, Zamboanga del Sur, MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAWAN, PANAY, SAMAR, SIASI.
species.notes: Luzon (Cagayan) to Palawan and Mindanao. Low and medium elevation forests. 0-976m.

species.citation: (Blume) Steud. , Nomencl. Bot. (Steudel), 2nd Ed. 2 (1841) 368 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 195; ≡ Tradescantia thyrsiflora Blume , Enum. Pl. Javae (1827) 6; Type: from Indonesia. = Pollia philippinensis Elmer , LPB 1 (1908) 275; Type: Elmer 9162, Luzon: Tayabas, Lucban, May-1907.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls to Java and Sulawesi. BALABAC, BASILAN, CAMIGUIN (photos), CEBU, LEYTE, LUZON: Abra, Albay, Apayao, Aurora (photos), Bulacan (photos), Cagayan, Camarines Sur (photos), Laguna, Quezon, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Bukidnon, Cotabato, Davao Occidental, Davao Oriental, Davao de Oro (photos), Davao del Sur, Misamis Oriental (photos), Zamboanga del Norte, MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAWAN, PANAY, POLILLO, SAMAR.
species.notes: Luzon (Cagayan to Sorsogon) to Palawan and Mindanao. Lowland and medium elevation forests. 150-2500m.

species.citation: Hassk. , Commelin. Ind. (1870) 56.
species.notes: Note by P.B. Pelser (1-Apr-14): the name Pollia sumatrana Hassk. has been incorrectly applied to Philippine plants according to LaFrankie (pers. comm. 2014).

species.citation: (Blume) Faden , Phytologia 37 (1977) 480; ≡ Commelina scaberrima Blume , Enum. Pl. Javae (1827) 4.
species.distribution: Himalayas to Malesia, Philippines. LUZON: Rizal.

species.citation: (Seem.) Faden , Biosyst. Study Gen. Aneilema (Commelinaceae) 2 (1975) 395; ≡ Aneilema vitiense Seem. , Bonplandia (1861) 260 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 197. = Aneilema vitiense var. petiolata C.B.Clarke , Monogr. Phan. 3 (1881) 220 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 197.
species.distribution: Pacific Ocean, Philippines. CEBU, LEYTE, LUZON: Benguet, Cagayan, Laguna, Nueva Vizcaya, Quezon, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Misamis Oriental (photos), MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAWAN, PANAY.
species.notes: Luzon (Cagayan) to Palawan and Mindanao, in most islands and provinces. Lowlands and medium elevation, ascending to 800m (photos) to 1400m, shaded ravines along creek gullies. 700m.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 5 November 2023

species.citation: Sw. , Prodr. (Swartz) (1788) 57; ≡ Rhoeo spathacea (Sw.) Stearn , Baileya 5 (1957) 195. = Rhoeo discolor (L'Hér.) Hance , Ann. Bot. Syst. 3 (1853) 660 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 139; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 195; ≡ Tradescantia discolor L'Hér. , Sert. Angl. (1789) 8.
species.distribution: America. DINAGAT (photos).
species.notes: Now widely cultivated in the tropics, easily escaping and often becoming naturalized.

species.citation: Bosse , Vollst. Handb. Bl.-gärtn., 2nd Ed. 4 (1849) 655; Type: not known, based on material cultivated in Europe. var. zebrina; Delos Angeles et al. in Ramamoorthy et al., Plant Diversity in Biocultural Landscapes (2023) 224. = Tradescantia pendula (Schnizl.) D.R.Hunt, Kew Bull. 36 (1981) 197; ≡ Zebrina pendula Schnizl., Bot. Zeitung (Berlin) 7 (1849) 870.; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 140; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 199. Distribution: Neotropics. SAMAR. Notes: Introduced from tropical America and cultivated as an ornamental, now more or less established in coconut groves, etc. at medium elevation. Wandering Jew. Naturalized.
species.distribution: Neotropics. MINDORO (photos), SAMAR.
species.notes: Invasive species (CABI 2017).

species.citation: (Rose) D.R.Hunt , Kew Bull. 30 (1975) 452; ≡ Setcreasea pallida Rose , Contr. U.S. Natl. Herb. 13 (1911) 294. = Setcreasea purpurea Boom , Acta Bot. Neerl. 4 (1955) 167; Type: from Mexico.
species.distribution: America.
species.notes: Widely grown as an ornamental in the tropics. Not naturalized?

species.citation: (Panigrahi) Faden , Smithsonian Contr. Bot. 76 (1991) 153; ≡ Dictyospermum philippense Panigrahi , Phytologia 29 (1974) 338. = Aneilema humile Merr. , PJS 13c (1918) 4, nom. illeg., non Warb. ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 196; Type: Merrill 10459.
